# Imbas OS

> Local-first home base for AI agents: artifacts, memory/context, living wiki pages, Runledger audit history, context packs, snapshots, mobile companion, and human approval flows.

Imbas OS is private-preview software. Do not represent it as public-ready until the documentation and fresh-system 1.0 gates pass and Johnathan explicitly approves release.

## Core links

- [README](README.md): single canonical human+AI-readable project entrypoint.
- [Agent rules](AGENTS.md): repository rules, safety boundaries, and verification expectations for autonomous agents.
- [Actionable workflows](skill.md): task workflows for agents using or modifying Imbas OS.
- [Documentation index](docs/index.md): docs map and 1.0 documentation standards.
- [Implementation status](docs/implementation-status.md): current evidence matrix, blockers, and verification gates.
- [Fresh-system 1.0 gate](docs/release/fresh-system-1.0-gate.md): required clean-system release gate.
- [Documentation 1.0 gate](docs/release/documentation-1.0-gate.md): docs readiness gate.

## Setup and use

- [Local development setup](docs/setup/local-development.md): clone, install, run, verify, troubleshoot.
- [Android companion setup](docs/setup/android-companion.md): APK build/install, pairing, diagnostics, mobile flows.
- [OpenClaw integration setup](docs/setup/openclaw-integration.md): private-preview OpenClaw/Conduit integration.
- [User guide](docs/how-to/use-imbas-os.md): desktop, Agent Console, artifacts, wiki, Runledger, Android.
- [Lorekeeper review workflow](docs/how-to/lorekeeper-review.md): proposals, diffs, apply, snapshots, restore.
- [Verification gates](docs/ops/verification.md): test/build/smoke/package gates and live checks.
- [Troubleshooting](docs/troubleshooting.md): common private-preview problems.
- [Known limitations](docs/known-limitations.md): explicit non-public-ready gaps.
- [Glossary](docs/glossary.md): subsystem terminology.

## Architecture

- [Subsystem map](docs/architecture/subsystems.md): Memsocket, Artifact Vault, Lorekeeper, Conduit, Runledger, Sanctum, Atlas, SyncCore, Desktop, Mobile, CLI.
- [AI-first OS principle](docs/architecture/ai-first-os.md): UI/CLI/mobile/agents use stable module contracts.
- [Module selection](docs/architecture/module-selection.md): optional install profiles and module enablement.
- [Memory migration](docs/architecture/memory-migration.md): MemPalace to Imbas/Memsocket staged migration.
- [Connector protocol](docs/connectors/protocol.md): Conduit connector/API design.
- [Sanctum](docs/sanctum/agent-secret-vault.md): secret/capability boundary.
- [Threat model](docs/threat-model.md): generated artifact security model.

## Current private-preview proof

- Electron + React + TypeScript desktop workbench.
- Local artifact/wiki vault and sandboxed generated HTML replay.
- Conduit loopback/private API with durable JSONL records.
- OpenClaw private-preview dispatch through constrained local CLI adapter.
- Lorekeeper proposal-first wiki review, guarded managed-block apply, snapshots, restore.
- Runledger audit timeline.
- Android Kotlin/Compose companion with pairing, QR prefill, Keystore token storage, scoped reads/actions, diagnostics, filtering, capture, and voice-dictation drafts.
- Private-preview package/restore verification.

## Safety and release boundaries

- No public release/publishing/announcement without explicit approval.
- Do not remove MemPalace until migration criteria pass and Johnathan approves.
- Do not expose raw secrets in artifacts, wiki, logs, Runledger, memory, or context packs.
- Generated HTML sandbox/network-deny defaults must remain protected.
- Tailnet HTTP is for scoped mobile reads/actions; sensitive apply/restore/dispatch paths stay desktop-guarded.
